title: Gently Comforting Middle Eastern Mung Bean Sprouts Musakhan
description: A floral and shaggy Middle Eastern dish featuring mung bean sprouts, made with a shio-koji cure and low-calorie friendly ingredients, served in a salt block vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the shio-koji, sumac, and olive oil.
Add the mung bean sprouts to the bowl and toss to coat with the shio-koji mixture.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the sliced onion.
Cook the onion for 5 minutes, or until it is lightly browned and caramelized.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the cooked onion and garlic to the bowl with the mung bean sprouts and toss to combine.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the mixture to a salt block vessel and bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
Remove the musakhan from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
